title: Mutton Karahi

seo.title: Mutton Karahi Recipe: Authentic Pakistani Dinner Dish
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Mutton Karahi with our authentic Pakistani recipe. Learn to make this flavorful, spicy dinner dish step-by-step at home. Perfect for any occasion!
lowCompetitionKeywords: Mutton Karahi with tomatoes and ginger
h2Headings: Tips for the Best Mutton Karahi
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) about Mutton Karahi
seo.imageAlt: A close-up of a sizzling plate of authentic Pakistani Mutton Karahi, garnished with fresh ginger and green chilies.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the rich, spicy flavors of Pakistan with this traditional Mutton Karahi, perfect for a hearty dinner.

description: Mutton Karahi is a quintessential Pakistani dish, celebrated for its rich, spicy flavors and tender meat cooked in a wok-like pan. This traditional recipe brings you the authentic taste of slow-cooked mutton, fresh tomatoes, ginger, and green chilies. It's a truly satisfying meal, perfect for a family dinner or special gathering.

instructions.title: Sear the Mutton
instructions.text: Heat oil or ghee in a large karahi or heavy-bottomed pot over high heat. Add the mutton pieces and sear them until they are lightly browned on all sides. This helps to lock in the juices and develop flavor.

instructions.title: Cook with Aromatics
instructions.text: Add the sliced onion (if using) and cook until translucent. Then, add the ginger garlic paste and continue to cook for 2-3 minutes until fragrant, ensuring it doesnt burn. Stir well to coat the mutton.

instructions.title: Add Spices and Tomatoes
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to medium. Add red chili powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, and salt. Stir for a minute. Immediately add the diced or pureed tomatoes. Mix thoroughly, cover the pot, and let it cook on medium-low heat until the tomatoes soften and release their water.

instructions.title: Tenderize the Mutton
instructions.text: Once the tomatoes have softened, mash them gently with the back of your spoon. Add about 1 cup of water, bring to a simmer, then cover and cook on low heat until the mutton is tender. This can take 30-45 minutes depending on the meat. Check periodically and add more water if needed.

instructions.title: Incorporate Yogurt and Chilies
instructions.text: When the mutton is tender and most of the water has evaporated, add the whisked yogurt and slit green chilies. Increase the heat to medium and cook, stirring continuously, until the yogurt is fully incorporated and the oil separates from the masala. This step is crucial for the karahi texture.

instructions.title: Finish and Garnish
instructions.text: Stir in the garam masala powder. Cook for another 2-3 minutes. Garnish generously with julienned fresh ginger and chopped fresh coriander leaves. Serve hot directly from the karahi or transfer to a serving dish.

tips: Always use a heavy-bottomed karahi or pot for even cooking and to prevent sticking. The slow cooking helps the mutton become incredibly tender.
For a richer flavor, use mutton with some bone-in pieces. Adjust the spice level by increasing or decreasing green chilies and red chili powder according to your preference.
Mutton Karahi is best served immediately, hot off the stove. Pair it with fresh naan, tandoori roti, or plain rice for a complete Pakistani dining experience.
article: Mutton Karahi is a beloved Pakistani Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
